title: Text Localization: EAST and CRAFT
concept: Text localization models like EAST and CRAFT find where text exists in an image, outputting word-level or character-level bounding regions.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the difference between text localization and text recognition?

options.text: Localization finds WHERE text is; recognition transcribes WHAT the text says.
Localization is done on CPUs; recognition is done on GPUs.

exercises.explain: Correct! Localization outputs coordinates of text boxes, while recognition translates the cropped pixels into strings.

cards.title: Text is not a cat
cards.body: If you want to detect objects like dogs and cars, you draw square bounding boxes. But text behaves differently: a single word is short, a sentence is long, and street signs can be curved or tilted. Standard object detectors struggle with text. To solve this, researchers built specialized text locators.

cards.title: EAST and CRAFT
cards.body: EAST (Efficient and Accurate Scene Text Detector) predicts text regions and coordinates in a single step, outputting rotated bounding boxes. CRAFT (Character Region Awareness for Text Detection) focuses on character-level detection, predicting both region scores (is this a character?) and affinity scores (are these characters linked?).

cards.title: Boxes vs. Lego chains
cards.body: EAST is like placing single rectangular boxes over words: it works great for clean, straight blocks of text. CRAFT is like building a chain of Lego blocks: it finds individual characters and links them together, allowing it to trace curved, irregular text chains easily.

cards.title: License plate readers
cards.body: Automatic toll cameras use text localization. They run models like EAST to isolate the rectangular region of the license plate in a car photo, ignoring the rest of the vehicle, before running recognition on the plate crop.

cards.title: YOLO is not ideal for text
cards.body: You might think you can just use YOLO for text detection. While possible, YOLO boxes are axis-aligned (flat rectangles) and struggle with tilted or curved text. Specialized OCR detectors are optimized to output rotated boxes (quadrilaterals) or character maps.

cards.title: EAST vs. CRAFT outputs
cards.body: How the two localization models detect text:

left.text:
Outputs rotated quadrilaterals
Fast, single-stage prediction
Struggles with curved text

right.text:
Outputs region/affinity scores
Traces arbitrary, curved text
Highly robust to handwriting

exercises.prompt: Which text localization model predicts character positions and character-connection affinity scores?
exercises.explain: CRAFT computes character region maps and affinity scores to link adjacent characters together.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Standard object detectors (like YOLO) are always the optimal choice for detecting tilted and curved scene text.
exercises.explain: False. Axis-aligned bounding boxes from standard detectors fail to frame tilted or curved text accurately; specialized text models are preferred.

exercises.sentence: EAST outputs rotated bounding boxes (quadrilaterals), whereas CRAFT evaluates character-level ___ scores.
exercises.explain: Affinity scores in CRAFT indicate the probability that two characters belong to the same word.

exercises.prompt: Which output represents CRAFT text localization?

options.text: A heatmap showing coordinates of individual characters and connections between them
A cropped square showing a person's face

exercises.explain: CRAFT outputs character probability grids (region scores) and connection maps (affinity scores).

exercises.prompt: You are building an OCR scanner for curved water bottles. The text wraps around the cylindrical surface, creating distorted, curved lines. Which detector should you choose and why?

options.text: CRAFT, because it detects individual characters and calculates affinity scores, allowing it to trace curved and distorted text strings.
EAST, because it forces straight, axis-aligned boxes that ignore curvature.
An image classifier, to classify the bottle volume.

exercises.explain: Exactly! CRAFT's character-based approach is designed specifically for curved, irregular scene text, as it doesn't assume text lines are straight boxes. Outstanding! Next: transcribing those cropped text regions.
